title: Computer Systems Security 6.5660 / 6.858
area: systems security, operating system security, secure design
useFor: Main security engineering backbone: threat models, secure systems, privilege separation, crypto protocols, web/mobile security.
notes: Free OCW material. Strong masters-level course; use papers/labs as stretch material.

title: Web Security Academy
area: web security, penetration testing, application security
useFor: Hands-on legal labs for SQLi, XSS, CSRF, auth, SSRF, request smuggling, JWT, GraphQL and modern web vulns.
notes: Use Burp Suite Community Edition. Stay inside the provided labs only.

title: Architecture 1001: x86-64 Assembly
area: assembly, low-level computing, reverse engineering foundation
useFor: Foundation for C/assembler, OS internals, exploit development and reverse engineering.
notes: Large course; do slowly. Good fit for Matt's electronics/instrumentation brain.

title: OpenSecurityTraining course catalogue
area: reverse engineering, secure coding, malware analysis, exploits, crypto
useFor: Pick modules: Intro Secure Coding, Vulnerability Assessment, Pcap Analysis, Malware Dynamic Analysis, Reverse Engineering.
notes: Older mirror points to OST2 where possible; still useful catalogue.

title: Free Cybersecurity Education Courses
area: network security, cloud security, SOC fundamentals
useFor: Beginner-friendly structured units for network security, cloud security and SOC concepts.
notes: Requires free Beacon account. Good quick wins after work.

title: Incident Response project and SP 800-61 Rev. 3
useFor: Build incident response playbooks and map response to CSF 2.0 functions.
notes: Use as professional reference for portfolio-quality IR plan.

title: DFIR Training catalogue
area: digital forensics, incident response, Autopsy, Cyber Triage
useFor: DFIR process, Autopsy basics, insider threat workshops and case-style investigations.
notes: Mix of free and paid. Use free workshops first.

title: Autopsy Digital Forensics Platform
useFor: Install and practice evidence ingestion, timelines, keywords, hashes and report generation.
notes: Open-source forensic platform. Use legal practice images only.

title: OWASP WebGoat
useFor: Local vulnerable web app for legal practice in appsec concepts.
notes: Run locally in a controlled lab environment.

title: OWASP Top 10
useFor: Baseline risk model for web app security reviews and capstone reports.
notes: Use for report structure and risk language.

title: Pre Security / Cyber Security learning paths
area: guided labs, Linux, networking, SOC, pentesting
useFor: Beginner-to-intermediate hands-on practice when you want guided rooms.
notes: Some free, some paid. Good for momentum.

title: Cybersecurity modules
useFor: Deeper hands-on practice after PortSwigger basics.

title: Cloud security labs
area: cloud security, IAM, logging, cloud operations
useFor: Cloud security lab practice and IAM/logging concepts.

title: Security, compliance, and identity training
area: cloud security, identity, SOC, Microsoft security stack
useFor: Career-friendly material for Azure/Microsoft security roles.
notes: Good if targeting security analyst or cloud security jobs.

title: Security+ free videos
useFor: Fast review of broad security concepts when a masters topic feels too abstract.
notes: Not masters-level by itself; good foundation filler.
